info.title: SCD Wellness — Mobile App API
info.description:
REST API for the SCD Wellness Flutter app.

Two user roles:
- **patient** — mobile app (iOS + Android)
- **doctor** — web dashboard

All protected routes require `Authorization: Bearer {token}`.
Tokens are issued by the login/register endpoints and scoped per role.

tags.name: Patient Auth
tags.description: Register, login (email or username), OTP password reset

tags.name: Patient Profile
tags.description: Personal info, medical info, emergency contacts, FCM token

tags.name: Patient Device
tags.description: Wearable device registration and sync status

tags.name: Patient Health Tracker
tags.description: Single aggregated endpoint for the Health Tracker screen — latest heart rate, medication logs, and symptoms in one call

tags.name: Patient Vitals
tags.description: Vitals sync from JCVital 2208A band and history queries

tags.name: Patient Alerts
tags.description: Alerts triggered by the server-side alert engine

tags.name: Patient Symptoms
tags.description: Symptom tracking — log, edit, history, daily stats

tags.name: Patient Medications
tags.description: Medication plans, daily schedule log, mark taken/missed

tags.name: Patient Triggers
tags.description: Trigger frequency and co-occurrence analysis derived from symptom data

tags.name: Patient Trends
tags.description: Aggregated health trends, smart insights, and care team alerts

identifier.description: Email address or username — either works

fcm_token.description: Refreshes push notification token on login

post.summary: Sign in with Google (Firebase) / Apple / Facebook
post.description:
**For mobile apps using Firebase Auth SDK (Google Sign-In via Firebase):** set `provider` to `firebase` and pass the Firebase ID token. The backend verifies the token cryptographically — no extra config needed on the client.

**For direct OAuth flows:** use `google`, `apple`, or `facebook` and pass the OAuth access token.

provider.description: Use `firebase` when signing in via the Firebase Auth SDK (recommended for mobile). Use `google`/`apple`/`facebook` for direct OAuth flows.

token.description: Firebase ID token (when provider=firebase) or OAuth access token (for other providers). Get this from the Firebase Auth SDK after the user completes Google Sign-In.

fcm_token.description: Optional — device push notification token. Saves an extra API call if passed here at login time.

422.description: Google account has no email address

post.summary: Step 1 — Request a 6-digit OTP via email
post.description: Always returns success to prevent email enumeration. OTP expires after 10 minutes.

200.description: OTP sent (or silently ignored if email not found)

post.summary: Step 2 — Verify OTP, receive a reset_token
Step 3 — Set new password using reset_token

put.summary: Change password while logged in (Settings screen)
Update genotype, blood type, and conditions

post.description: Adds a single emergency contact to the patient's list. Maximum 5 contacts total — returns 422 if the limit is already reached.

delete.description: Deletes a single emergency contact by its ID. The `id` is returned when adding a contact or in the profile response.

post.description:
Send the image as `multipart/form-data` with field name `avatar`.

Accepted formats: JPG, JPEG, PNG, WEBP. Max size: 5MB.

The previous avatar is automatically deleted from storage. The response returns the new public URL which is also saved to the profile.

avatar.description: Image file — JPG, JPEG, PNG or WEBP, max 5MB

422.description: Not an image / exceeds 5MB / wrong format

put.description: Call this on every app launch to keep the push token fresh.

put.summary: Update one or more privacy/notification toggles

post.summary: Register or re-pair the connected wearable band
post.description:
Registers a new band or re-pairs an existing one.

**Replacement band:** if the `device_id` is different from any previously registered device, all existing active devices for this patient are automatically deactivated before the new one is created. Historical readings from the old band are preserved.

**Re-pairing same band:** if the `device_id` already exists for this patient, it is simply updated (no deactivation occurs).

device_id.description: Unique hardware ID from the band

get.summary: Get status of all registered devices
Health Tracker screen — all data in one call
get.description: Returns the latest heart rate readings, medication logs, and symptom entries for the authenticated patient. **No date filtering is applied** — always returns the most recent records available, regardless of how old they are. Use this to populate the Health Tracker screen instead of making 3 separate calls.

heart_rate.description: Latest 10 heart rate readings from the band, newest first

medication_logs.description: Latest 10 medication plans (most recently added first) with today's status. Populated as soon as a medication is added — no need to mark taken/missed first.

symptoms.description: Latest 10 symptom entries, newest logged_at first

post.summary: Sync a batch of readings from the wearable
post.description:
Send a **bare JSON array** — no wrapper object.

The active device is resolved automatically from the patient's account (no `device_id` needed).
Returns **202 Accepted** immediately; the batch is processed asynchronously.
Fields with value `0` / `0.0` / `""` are ignored — only non-zero sensor readings are stored.

**Blood pressure:** both `highBP` and `lowBP` must be > 0 to be stored.
**spo2Date / bpDate:** if empty, the main `timestamp` is used as the recorded time.

queued.description: Total individual readings queued (one per non-zero field per object)

404.description: No active band registered — call /device/register first

422.description: Body is not a valid JSON array, or a timestamp is missing

get.description: Returns paginated vital readings for the authenticated patient. Use `page` to navigate and `per_page` to control how many records come back per request (max 200, default 50). Use `type` to filter by reading type and `range` to control the time window.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by vital type. Omit to get all types.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Time window. Defaults to last 24 hours.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number. Starts at 1.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: Records per page. Max 200, default 50.

meta.description: Pagination info — use current_page + last_page to know when you've reached the end

current_page.description: The page you are currently on

last_page.description: Total number of pages — stop paginating when current_page === last_page

get.summary: Get alerts and smart insights for this patient

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter by alert source

message.example: Severe symptoms logged 3 days in a row.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search by title or body text

patch.summary: Mark a single notification as read

get.summary: Symptoms screen — chart + history in one call
get.description: Single endpoint for the Symptoms screen. Returns the chart breakdown, summary stats, and paginated symptom history — all filtered consistently. When you toggle `severity`, `mood`, or `range`, all three sections update together. The old `/symptoms/stats` and `/symptoms` endpoints still work — this just combines them.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Date window for both the chart and history list. Default is last 7 days.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Filter chart and history to only this severity label

parameters.name: mood
parameters.description: Filter chart and history to only this mood

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: History page number

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: History items per page (max 100, default 20)

total_in_range.description: Total symptoms matching the current filters within the range

chart.description: Per-day breakdown — only days with matching entries appear

notes.example: Came on after a long walk

get.description: Returns summary stats (total, days logged, avg severity) and a per-day breakdown for the selected range. Use `range` to control how many days the chart covers.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Chart range — defaults to last 7 days

get.description: Use `period` for a relative date range, or `date` for a specific day. `severity` filters by label (mild/moderate/severe). `period` takes priority over `date` if both are sent.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Relative date range filter

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Filter to a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD). Ignored if `period` is set.

parameters.name: severity
parameters.description: Filter by severity label

parameters.name: mood
parameters.description: Filter by mood

delete.description: Permanently deletes all symptom records for the authenticated patient. This action cannot be undone.

get.summary: Get a single symptom record + pattern insights
get.description: Returns full symptom detail plus a `pattern_insights` string — an auto-generated sentence describing how often the same symptom appeared this week and which triggers were most common. Use this to populate the Entry Details screen.

pattern_insights.example: Chest Pain appeared 3x this week. Cold Weather was a trigger in 2 of those entries.
pattern_insights.description: Auto-generated insight based on the last 7 days of entries with the same symptom name. Always present — never null.

put.description: Only editable once (edit_count must be 0). A second edit attempt returns 403.

200.description: Symptom updated — returns the updated symptom

403.description: Already edited once — further edits are not allowed

get.description: Returns two lists for a given date: `upcoming` (not yet logged) and `history` (taken or missed). When `filter=taken` or `filter=missed`, the `upcoming` list is empty — only matching history entries are returned.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date to fetch schedule for (defaults to today)

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter results. `all` = upcoming + full history. `taken` / `missed` = only matching history entries, upcoming is empty.

upcoming.description: Doses not yet logged for the requested date. Empty when filter=taken or filter=missed.

history.description: Doses that have been marked taken or missed.

get.description: Returns a paginated, filterable feed of all medication log entries. `taken` and `missed` come from actual log records; `upcoming` is dynamically generated from active medication schedules (today → end_date or today + 30 days if no end_date supplied). Sort order: newest/soonest first by `scheduled_at`.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status. Omit to return all three statuses.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Include entries with scheduled_at on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Include entries with scheduled_at on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Upcoming defaults to today + 30 days when omitted.

delete.description: Permanently deletes all medication plans and their associated log entries for the authenticated patient. This action cannot be undone.

today_status.description: Today's dose status. pending = not yet logged, taken = all doses taken, missed = all doses missed, partial = some taken/missed

201.description: Medication added — returns the created medication plan

200.description: Medication updated — returns the updated medication plan

delete.description: Also nulls out `medication_id` on any associated log entries so history is preserved.

post.summary: Mark a scheduled dose as taken

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Medication plan ID

post.summary: Mark a scheduled dose as missed

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Medication plan ID

get.description: Counts how often each trigger appears across the patient's symptom logs within the given period. Also detects pairs of triggers that frequently co-occur (Pattern Insights). Powers the **My Triggers** screen.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Time window to analyse

pattern_insights.description: Often appear together — occurred 4 times this period

get.description:
Returns trend data for the selected tab. Pass `filter` to match whichever tab the user taps.

| filter | Returns |
|---|---|
| `all` | symptoms + triggers + medication + mood |
| `pain` | most frequent symptoms + care team alert |
| `triggers` | top triggers + pattern insights + care team alert |
| `medication` | medication list with today_status + 30-day adherence stats + care team alert |
| `mood` | mood frequency breakdown + care team alert |

200.description: Trend data — shape varies by filter

data.care_team_alert: Severe Chest pain logged on Apr 18. Your care team has been notified.

pattern_insights.description: Often appear together — occurred 4 times this month

medication.care_team_alert: You missed 13 dose(s) in the last 30 days. Your care team has been notified.

data.mood_care_team_alert: Your mood has been stable this week. Your care team has been notified.

data.care_team_alert: Severe Chest pain logged on Apr 18. Your care team has been notified.

medication.care_team_alert: You missed 13 dose(s) in the last 30 days. Your care team has been notified.

data.mood_care_team_alert: Your mood has been stable this week. Your care team has been notified.

pattern_insights.description: Often appear together — occurred 4 times this month

data.care_team_alert: High Cold water trigger frequency detected. Your care team has been notified.

get.summary: List all patients assigned to this doctor
Get all alerts for a specific patient

post.summary: Add a clinical note to a patient record

content.example: Patient reported improvement after medication adjustment.

get.description: Returns total patients, active alert counts, and upcoming appointments.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: 1 = critical, 2 = warning

put.summary: Assign an alert to a doctor

get.summary: Vitals trends across all assigned patients
Alert counts by type, level, and status
Export vitals data as a CSV file
